edges.description: SP110 deficiency disrupts immune development and humoral differentiation.
SP110 deficiency is linked to the hepatic vascular injury arm of the syndrome.
edges.target: Hepatic sinusoidal and terminal venular occlusion

edges.description: Defective immune maturation produces recurrent bacterial and opportunistic infections.
edges.target: Combined immunodeficiency with impaired pathogen control

edges.description: Combined immunodeficiency predisposes to Pneumocystis and other opportunistic infections.
edges.source: Combined immunodeficiency with impaired pathogen control

edges.description: Opportunistic immune failure can culminate in Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia.
Ongoing vascular and parenchymal injury promotes fibrotic liver remodeling.
edges.source: Hepatic sinusoidal and terminal venular occlusion
edges.target: Hepatic stellate cell activation and fibrotic remodeling

edges.description: Activated hepatic mesenchymal cells contribute to irreversible fibrotic liver injury.
edges.source: Hepatic stellate cell activation and fibrotic remodeling
edges.target: Progressive hepatic fibrosis and organ dysfunction

nodes.description: Progressive hepatic vascular-fibrotic injury can culminate in hepatic failure during infancy.

evidence.reference_title: The first prenatal diagnosis for veno-occlusive disease and immunodeficiency syndrome, an autosomal recessive condition associated with mutations in SP110.
evidence.snippet: The proband in this report presented at three months of age with hepatomegaly hepatic failure and was found to have hypogammaglobulinemia.

nodes.description: Combined immunodeficiency is a defining immunologic feature of VODI.

evidence.snippet: Hepatic veno-occlusive disease with immunodeficiency (VODI) is characterized by (1) combined immunodeficiency

nodes.description: The immune defect in VODI leads to early severe infection susceptibility, including Pneumocystis and candidiasis, alongside profound antibody deficiency.
nodes.id: Combined immunodeficiency with impaired pathogen control

evidence.snippet: Bacterial and opportunistic infections including Pneumocystis jirovecii infection, mucocutaneous candidiasis, and enteroviral or cytomegalovirus infections occur.

nodes.description: SP110 deficiency impairs humoral and cellular immune maturation, with absent germinal centers and plasma cells despite circulating lymphocyte numbers that may be preserved.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the PML nuclear body protein Sp110 are associated with immunodeficiency and hepatic veno-occlusive disease.
evidence.snippet: an autosomal recessive disorder of severe hypogammaglobulinemia, combined T and B cell immunodeficiency, absent lymph node germinal centers, absent tissue plasma cells

nodes.description: Defibrotide is used for acute hepatic disease and prophylactically around HSCT in VODI.

evidence.snippet: MANAGEMENT: Targeted therapies: IVIG/SCIG; defibrotide for acute hepatic disease; HSCT with non-hepatotoxic conditioning therapy, preferably with defibrotide prophylaxis.

nodes.description: Early HSCT with non-hepatotoxic conditioning is a potentially curative treatment strategy for VODI.

evidence.snippet: Early hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) using non-hepatoxic drugs in conditioning and prophylactic defibrotide is potentially curative.

nodes.description: Hepatic disease in VODI is characterized by lobular vascular occlusion and sinusoidal/terminal venular injury that progresses to fibrosis and hepatic failure.
nodes.id: Hepatic sinusoidal and terminal venular occlusion

evidence.snippet: terminal hepatic lobular vascular occlusion and hepatic fibrosis manifesting as hepatomegaly and/or hepatic failure
A central pathogenic event is toxic destruction of hepatic sinusoidal endothelial cells (SEC), with sloughing and downstream occlusion of terminal hepatic venules.

nodes.description: Persistent hepatic vascular injury drives mesenchymal activation and liver-specific fibrotic remodeling, consistent with the shared fibrotic response module.
nodes.id: Hepatic stellate cell activation and fibrotic remodeling

evidence.snippet: terminal hepatic lobular vascular occlusion and hepatic fibrosis manifesting as hepatomegaly and/or hepatic failure

nodes.description: Severe hypogammaglobulinemia is a central laboratory and clinical feature of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the PML nuclear body protein Sp110 are associated with immunodeficiency and hepatic veno-occlusive disease.
evidence.snippet: an autosomal recessive disorder of severe hypogammaglobulinemia, combined T and B cell immunodeficiency

nodes.description: Intravenous or subcutaneous immunoglobulin replacement improves survival and infection control in affected infants.

evidence.snippet: In the past the prognosis for affected individuals was poor, with 100% mortality in the first year of life if unrecognized and untreated with intravenous or subcutaneous immunoglobulin (IVIG/SCIG)

nodes.description: Liver transplantation may be considered in severe hepatic failure, although complications can be substantial in this immunodeficient syndrome.

evidence.snippet: consider liver transplantation, although rate of complications may be high.

nodes.description: Profound combined immunodeficiency in VODI predisposes infants to severe opportunistic pulmonary infections, particularly Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia.

evidence.snippet: Bacterial and opportunistic infections including Pneumocystis jirovecii infection, mucocutaneous candidiasis, and enteroviral or cytomegalovirus infections occur.

nodes.description: Opportunistic Pneumocystis infection reflects the syndrome's combined immunodeficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Hepatic veno-occlusive disease with immunodeficiency (VODI): first reported case in the U.S. and identification of a unique mutation in Sp110.

nodes.description: Prophylaxis against Pneumocystis jirovecii is part of standard supportive care for affected infants with profound combined immunodeficiency.

evidence.snippet: Supportive care: Pneumocystis jirovecii prophylaxis; prompt treatment of infections with antibacterials, antivirals, or antifungals as indicated;

nodes.description: Progressive hepatic fibrosis drives hepatomegaly, ascites, and hepatic failure in advanced VODI.
nodes.id: Progressive hepatic fibrosis and organ dysfunction

evidence.snippet: terminal hepatic lobular vascular occlusion and hepatic fibrosis manifesting as hepatomegaly and/or hepatic failure

DIAGNOSIS/TESTING: The diagnosis of VODI is established in a proband who meets clinical diagnostic criteria or by identification of biallelic pathogenic variants in SP110 on molecular genetic testing.
SP110 | HGNC:5401 | hepatic veno-occlusive disease-immunodeficiency syndrome | MONDO:0009338 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: VODI is caused by pathogenic loss of SP110, a PML nuclear body-associated protein, establishing the initiating molecular lesion of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the PML nuclear body protein Sp110 are associated with immunodeficiency and hepatic veno-occlusive disease.
evidence.snippet: We describe mutations in the PML nuclear body protein Sp110 in the syndrome veno-occlusive disease with immunodeficiency
